news.summary: A conference call is set for Monday, May 12, 2025, at 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time in NEWTON, Mass., as declared by Karyopharm Therapeutics Inc. (Nasdaq: KPTI) on May 8, 2025. news.summary: On May 8, 2025, OUTFRONT Media Inc. (NYSE: OUT) revealed that its board of directors approved a quarterly cash dividend for the Company's common stock, set at $0.30 per share. This dividend will be disbursed on June 30, 2025, to shareholders who are recorded as of June 6.
news.headline: OUTFRONT Media (NYSE: OUT) declares $0.30/share quarterly dividend, payable on June 30, 2025, to shareholders of record on June 6.

news.summary: In the latest financial report, OUTFRONT Media Inc. disclosed revenues totaling $390.7 million and an operating income of $13.9 million. However, there was a net loss attributable to OUTFRONT Media Inc. amounting to $20.6 million. The adjusted OIBDA stood at $64.2 million, while the AFFO attributable to OUTFRONT Media Inc. was reported at $23.9 million. Shareholders can expect a quarterly dividend of $0.30 per share, scheduled to be paid out on June 30, 2025.
news.headline: OUTFRONT Media Q1 2025: $390.7M revenue, $13.9M operating income, $20.6M net loss, $64.2M adjusted OIBDA, $23.9M AFFO, $0.30 per share dividend.

news.summary: In the first quarter, Compass, Inc. reported a significant growth in revenue, with a 28.7% year-over-year increase. Organic revenue also saw a strong growth of 14.6% year-over-year. The company's quarterly market share increased by 125 basis points year-over-year to reach a record high of 6.0%, while organic market share grew by 82 basis points. Operating cash flow experienced remarkable growth, surging by 169% year-over-year to achieve a record $23.1 million in Q1.
news.headline: Compass, Inc. had a record Q1 in 2025, with revenue growing by 28.7% YoY to $23.1 million. Market share reached 6.0%.

news.summary: In Bloomington, Minnesota, SkyWater Technology (NASDAQ: SKYT), a leading technology realization partner, will be attending three investor conferences in the second quarter. The first conference, B. Riley Securities 25th Annual Investor Conference, is set for May 22, 2025, at The Ritz-Carlton in Marina Del Rey, California, with exclusive 1x1 meetings. The second conference, Craig-Hallum 22nd Annual Institutional Investor Conference, will be held on May 28, 2025, at the Depot Renaissance in Minneapolis, Minnesota, also featuring 1x1 sessions. SkyWater Technology's management will be participating in these events.
news.headline: SkyWater Technology (NASDAQ: SKYT) to attend 3 investor conferences in Q2: B. Riley Securities on May 22 and Craig-Hallum on May 28.

news.summary: Granite Ridge Resources, Inc. announced its first-quarter financial and operational performance for 2025. The company experienced a significant 23% increase in daily production, reaching 29,245 barrels of oil equivalent per day, with 50% of the production being oil. In terms of financials, Granite Ridge reported a net income of $9.8 million for the quarter, translating to earnings of $0.07 per diluted share. This marked a decrease from the previous year's net income of $16.2 million, or $0.12 per diluted share.
news.headline: Granite Ridge Resources, Inc. grew daily production by 23% to 29,245 Boe per day in Q1 2025, with net income $9.8 million.

news.summary: Willdan Group, Inc. (Nasdaq: WLDN) reported its financial results for the first quarter ending April 4, 2025. Highlights include a 24.4% increase in contract revenue to $152.4 million, a 23.8% rise in net revenue to $85.3 million, and a 59.3% growth in net income to $4.7 million. Additionally, adjusted EBITDA increased by 30.9% to $14.4 million. GAAP Diluted EPS rose by 52.4% to $0.32, while Adjusted Diluted EPS increased by 57.5% to $0.63. The company's executive management expressed their satisfaction with the results, stating they surpassed analyst expectations.
news.headline: Willdan Group's first quarter results show significant growth, with contract revenue up 24.4% to $152.4 million and net income up 59.3% to $4.7 million. Adjusted EBITDA increased by 30.9% to $14.4 million, while GAAP Diluted EPS rose by 52.4% to $0.32. Executive Management is positive about surpassing analyst expectations.

news.summary: In Durham, N.C., Wolfspeed, Inc. (NYSE: WOLF) revealed its fiscal 2025 third quarter results. Key financial points include: a consolidated revenue of $185 million, down from $201 million in comparison to the same period in fiscal 2024. Mohawk Valley Fab generated $78 million in revenue, a significant increase from $28 million. The GAAP gross margin saw a decrease to (12)% from 11%, while the non-GAAP gross margin dropped to 2% from 15%. The company did not specify further details but expressed the results in continuation of operations only, maintaining focus on revenue and margin shifts.
news.headline: Wolfspeed Inc. (NYSE: WOLF) reported Q3 2025 revenue of $185M, down from $201M in Q3 2024, with a negative gross margin.

news.summary: Oklahoma City-based Mach Natural Resources LP disclosed its financial and operational performance for the first quarter of 2025. The company, listed on NYSE as MNR, reported an average total net production of 80.9 Mboe/d during the period. Lease operating expenses stood at $6.69 per Boe. Furthermore, the company announced its quarterly cash distribution and reaffirmed its full-year 2025 outlook. Net income figures were also released by the company.
news.headline: Mach Natural Resources LP reported Q1 2025 results with 80.9 Mboe/d production and $0.79 cash distribution per common unit.

news.summary: Clean Energy Fuels Corp. released its first-quarter results for 2025, showing a revenue of $103.8 million as compared to $103.7 million in the same period of 2024. The net loss attributable to Clean Energy for Q1 2025 was $(135.0) million, or $(0.60) per share on a GAAP basis, a significant increase from $(18.4) million, or $(0.08) per share, reported for Q1 2024. The Q1 2025 net loss covers various financial aspects and developments within the company's operations.
news.headline: Clean Energy's Q1 2025 revenue hits $103.8 million, with 50.6 million RNG gallons sold. Net loss is $(135.0) million.

news.summary: In Hilliard, Ohio, Advanced Drainage Systems, Inc. (NYSE: WMS) revealed the acquisition of River Valley Pipe LLC, a Midwest-based pipe manufacturing firm. ADS, a key player in stormwater and onsite septic wastewater solutions, finalized the deal to bring River Valley Pipe, a privately-owned company, into their operations. Scott Barbour, the President and CEO of ADS, conveyed enthusiasm about this development, emphasizing the strategic importance of the move. This acquisition marks a significant step for both companies, with ADS solidifying its position further in the industry.
news.headline: Advanced Drainage Systems, Inc. has acquired River Valley Pipe, a Midwest pipe manufacturing company. - 90 characters

news.summary: The upcoming release of key findings from the crucial INFRONT-3 Phase 3 clinical study evaluating latozinemab for the treatment of FTD-GRN is scheduled to occur in the fourth quarter of 2025. This trial marks a significant milestone in the development of this therapy, potentially impacting the management of Frontotemporal Dementia associated with GRN mutations.
news.headline: Alector's Q1 2025 financial results released; pivotal trial data for latozinemab in FTD-GRN due in Q4 2025.

news.summary: PubMatic has recently revealed its financial performance for the first quarter of 2025. In this period, the company reported encouraging numbers, demonstrating its growth and stability in the market. Additionally, PubMatic's Board of Directors has approved a significant development by authorizing a $100 million expansion of the share repurchase program. This move indicates the company's confidence in its current financial standing and its commitment to providing value to its shareholders.
news.headline: PubMatic reports Q1 2025 financial results; Board approves $100M increase in share repurchase program.

news.summary: In the last quarter, the company reported a decrease in total net sales from $138.8 million to $137.1 million. Net income also saw a decline from $3.9 million to ($0.8) million compared to the same period in the previous year. EBITDA stood at $3.5 million, a drop from $9.6 million in the prior year's first quarter. Despite these figures, the company is actively pursuing its stock repurchase plan. Additionally, the Board of Directors has authorized a quarterly dividend of $0.14 per share.
news.headline: Superior Group reports Q1 2025 revenue at $137.1M, net loss of ($0.8M), EBITDA at $3.5M, and $0.14 quarterly dividend.

news.summary: An announcement was made that a new record in daily COVID-19 cases was reported in the country, with a total of 10,000 new infections identified in the past 24 hours. This marks the highest number of cases since the beginning of the pandemic. Authorities have raised concerns over the surge in cases and emphasized the importance of following safety measures to curb the spread of the virus. The increase in infections has put a strain on the healthcare system, with hospitals reaching full capacity. The government is urging the public to adhere to guidelines, including wearing masks, practicing social distancing, and getting vaccinated to help control the outbreak.
news.headline: BlackLine enhances AI capabilities to speed up modern financial operations, adapting for the future.

As a pioneer in fertility control products for animal pest management, SenesTech is the sole producer of EPA-approved rodent birth control items. The company, listed on NASDAQ as SNES, revealed its quarterly earnings without directly mentioning specific figures.
news.headline: SenesTech's Q1 2025: 40% revenue growth in Evolve™, gross profit margins of 65%, moving towards profitability.

news.summary: In the initial quarter of 2025, revenue reached $534.2 million, marking a 7.4% decrease. The net income stood at $3.2 million, equivalent to 0.6% of total revenue ($13.6 million or 2.6% of revenue on a non-GAAP basis). Adjusted EBITDA amounted to $56.4 million, representing 10.6% of revenue. The company reaffirms its full-year 2025 outlook. The location of the report is Austin, Texas, while the data was released on May 8.
news.headline: TTEC's Q1 2025 revenue: $534.2M, Net Income: $3.2M, Adjusted EBITDA: $56.4M. Outlook for 2025 reiterated.

news.summary: In Birmingham, Michigan on May 8, 2025, OneStream, Inc. (Nasdaq: OS), a prominent enterprise Finance management platform, is making waves. They are at the forefront of revolutionizing the Office of the CFO by consolidating core finance and operational tasks. This includes financial close, consolidation, reporting, planning, and other essential functions.
news.headline: OneStream (Nasdaq: OS) reports Q1 2025 financial results, focusing on key enterprise finance management services.

news.summary: The BEACH301 trial investigating TYRA-300 for Pediatric Achondroplasia (ACH) is now accepting participants. Additionally, dosing for patients has commenced in the SURF431 study of TYRA-430 for hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC). The company reported having $318.9 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities in Q1 2025, ensuring funding until at least 2027.
news.headline: Tyra Biosciences Q1 2025: Enroll BEACH301, SURF431 trials; $318.9M cash; runway till 2027.

news.summary: Today at the annual stockholders meeting of WEC Energy Group (NYSE: WEC) in Milwaukee on May 8, 2025, Chairman of the Board Gale Klappa and President/CEO Scott Lauber praised the company's outstanding performance over the year. Key indicators such as customer satisfaction showed remarkable growth. The company's robust outcomes were underscored in various aspects, demonstrating substantial progress in achieving its goals.
news.headline: WEC Energy Group reports exceptional year at annual meeting with chairman and CEO praising company's performance.

news.summary: Progress is being made towards launching Phase 2 studies in ulcerative colitis (UC) and rheumatoid arthritis (RA) by mid-year, with plans for more than 7 proof-of-concept results in 2026 and 2027. Extended follow-up data from the Phase 1 trial of SPY001 indicates that the molecule is well tolerated.
news.headline: Spyre Therapeutics on track for Phase 2 studies in UC & RA, with 7+ proof-of-concept readouts in 2026 & 2027. SPY001 well tolerated, with extended Phase 1 data.
